edges.evidence_quote: Until 2017, it was an ally of the Primeiro Comando da Capital, but they broke with their former allies in Teresina, sparking an intense war.
edges.context: Bonde dos 40 was allied with PCC until 2017, then broke and went to war.

edges.evidence_quote: Until 2017, it was an ally of the Primeiro Comando da Capital, but they broke with their former allies in Teresina, sparking an intense war.
edges.context: After breaking alliance, Bonde dos 40 and PCC are in conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: forming alliances with Comando Vermelho (CV) and Amigos dos Amigos (ADA).
edges.context: Bonde dos 40 formed an alliance with Comando Vermelho.

edges.evidence_quote: It was also reported that same year that it entered into a war with Comando Vermelho to dominate the Vila Conceição neighborhood in southern São Luís.
edges.context: In 2019, Bonde dos 40 entered a war with Comando Vermelho.

edges.evidence_quote: forming alliances with Comando Vermelho (CV) and Amigos dos Amigos (ADA).
edges.context: Bonde dos 40 formed an alliance with Amigos dos Amigos.

edges.evidence_quote: the group became known for instigating riots in prisons and in the Pedrinhas Penitentiary Complex against the Primeiro Comando do Maranhão (PCM), and for carrying out attacks on the streets, resulting in the deaths of over 100 inmates Since then, PCM and B40 have been violently battling for control over stilt neighborhoods and peripheral communities, causing dozens of deaths and injuries.
edges.context: Bonde dos 40 has been violently battling PCM for control.
